focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benny nods to Scotty before going in; the block empties under streetlights that resemble baseballs, evoking Scotty's envy of the treehouse even before he knew the boys.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Benny, the last to go inside, sees Scotty watching and nods to him.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benny initiates non-verbal contact with Scotty by nodding.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benny goes into his house after nodding.
evidence_quotes: FROM BENNY'S PORCH Benny is the last to go in. He sees Scotty watching. So, he nods... just a little.

relation_updates: Initiates mutual recognition with Scotty

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A boy discovers a room filled with baseball memorabilia, realizing it's a shrine to the sport; inspired, he decides baseball is the way to connect with the others.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: New kid who has been observing the neighborhood for a week
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After a week of watching, Benny decides that baseball is the best way to get in with the neighborhood group.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To join the neighborhood baseball group
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Is preparing to approach the group by oiling his glove and staring at Scotty's house from his window.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Has not yet approached the group
evidence_quotes: after a week of watching... I figured baseball seemed like the best way to get in with them.
BENNY'S in bed, oiling his glove, staring out his window at Scotty's house.
Taped to his footboard is the most important picture of all... a picture of The Babe.

relation_updates: Targets the sandlot gang for social connection
status_updates: Develops integration strategy based on baseball
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Inspired by baseball memorabilia and heroes like Babe Ruth

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benny reveals Babe Ruth was called 'The Babe' because he cried when bullied at the orphanage, evoking empathy and leaving the boys silent as he exits.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Corrects group's mockery by teaching empathy through a historical story
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tells the story of Babe Ruth's childhood bullying to evoke empathy and challenge the group's laughter
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To make the group empathize with the bullied child, relating it to Scotty's situation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Exits the sandlot, leaving the group silent and having learned a lesson
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the group will change their behavior towards Scotty based on the lesson
evidence_quotes: BENNY I bet not one of you knows how The Babe got his nickname.
BENNY (CONT'D) How ya think that kid just felt?
Benny exits the sandlot, leaving the others with a lesson learned.

relation_updates: Asserts moral authority over the group
Teaches lesson about empathy through baseball history
status_updates: Establishes himself as group's moral compass
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Assertive leader who uses baseball history to impart moral lessons

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benny introduces Scotty to the silent, tough gang members, who eye him warily before accepting him as the ninth player needed to form their team.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Team assembler and leader
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benny introduces Scotty to the gang by naming each member and declares him the ninth player, stating they now have a team.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To form a complete baseball team with nine players
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benny has added Scotty as the ninth player, and the team is now complete as per his declaration.
evidence_quotes: BENNY He's gonna play with us. He makes nine. Now we got a team.

relation_updates: Benny includes Scotty in the gang
Benny establishes himself as the decision-maker for the team
status_updates: Benny is the leader who brings in new members
Team now has nine players
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Leader who makes decisions for the team and integrates new members

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benny coaches Scotty in batting, using bottle caps as targets; Scotty improves rapidly, hitting every one as the sun sets.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Coach and mentor to Scotty
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benny coaches Scotty on batting stance and swing using bottle caps, leading to Scotty hitting all caps by sunset.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Scotty is hitting all bottle caps by sunset.
evidence_quotes: Benny coaches Scotty.
Correct batting stance and swing.
Benny thumb-flicks bottle caps at Scotty from a coffee can full.
But soon Scotty's tagging every one of them.

relation_updates: Establishes mentorship with Scotty
status_updates: Assumes coaching role in the group

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The gang warns Smalls and Scotty against retrieving the ball, citing 'The Beast' beyond the fence; Scotty is urged to peek through a hole to see the danger, heightening tension.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Group leader enforcing boundary
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benny stops Scotty from entering the Beast's yard, ends the game due to the lost ball, and instructs Scotty to peek through the fence to observe the danger.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Prevent group access to the Beast's yard to maintain safety.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Game is abandoned; ball remains in the yard; Scotty is directed to look through the fence.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The lost ball is irretrievable and the Beast remains unseen.
evidence_quotes: You can't go back there, Smalls.
Game's over. We'll get a new one tomorrow. Just forget it. We'll never see it again.
Go over there, real slow, and be quiet. Don't touch the fence, just peek through that hole in the green stuff... go on.

relation_updates: Asserts authority over Scotty
Reinforces group consensus about the danger
status_updates: Established as rule-enforcer regarding the Beast
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Authoritative protector enforcing rules for group safety.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scotty collapses, prompting the boys to help; realizing the signed baseball is lost, they resolve to replace it. Benny leads efforts to collect bottles for 98 cents to buy a new ball, forging a fake Babe Ruth signature.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Team leader who catches the ball and initially reassures Scotty after it is lost.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Learns the lost ball is a Babe Ruth autograph, joining the gang in recognizing the high-stakes crisis.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To replace or retrieve the lost ball.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benny and the gang are united in knowing the Babe Ruth ball is in the Beast's possession and must be retrieved.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to retrieve the ball from the Beast's yard.
evidence_quotes: He tosses the ball to BENNY who catches it. BENNY Bitchin'. Your ball, your ups.
BENNY It was a great shot, but forget about it... game's over. We'll get another ball.

relation_updates: Benny's relationship with Scotty deepens as he understands the personal stakes; the gang unites in shared crisis.
status_updates: Benny becomes involved in a high-stakes conflict requiring group leadership.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Babe Ruth tells Benny legends never die, urging him to follow his heart before a noise from the closet interrupts, leaving Benny to face his challenge with newfound resolve.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Benny is visited by Babe Ruth's ghost and receives direct advice to retrieve the baseball.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Babe Ruth appears from Benny's closet, motivates him to get the baseball back, and Benny confesses his fear of the Beast in the backyard.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Retrieve the signed baseball from the backyard.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benny is encouraged by Babe Ruth and resolves to face his fear, though he has not yet acted.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Benny's fear of the Beast persists, but he is now motivated to confront it.
evidence_quotes: BENNY We can't get it back, Babe. (BEAT) I'm sorry.
BENNY There's a Beast back there.
Benny comes off the wall.

status_updates: Gains motivation from Babe Ruth
Acknowledges fear of the Beast

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benny, after a moment of hesitation and a final thumbs-up to Scotty, decides to follow his heart and jumps over the fence, embracing his destiny.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Benny commits to jumping the fence
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benny puts on P.F. Flyers, reassures Scotty with a thumbs-up, and jumps the fence.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benny is over the fence
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Facing what lies beyond the fence
evidence_quotes: Laces on brand new P.F. Flyers.
BENNY (PREDESTINED) Yeah I do, Smalls. I have to.
Swings himself onto THE TOP OF THE FENCE
Gives Scotty "Thumbs Up."

relation_updates: Reassures Scotty with a thumbs-up
status_updates: Equipped with P.F. Flyers

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benny retrieves Babe Ruth's signed ball from a ferocious dog, narrowly escaping as the chain-restrained beast lunges, snapping at him before Benny vaults over the fence.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Retriever of the Babe Ruth signed ball from The Beast
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Grabs the ball from the dirt, re-secures it after a slip, and vaults over the fence to escape The Beast.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benny has the ball and is safe outside the fence.
evidence_quotes: Benny goes for the ball.
The ball slips from Benny's fingers. Re-grabs it — shoves the ball in his teeth, freeing his hands for the leap up the fence.
BENNY springs off the ground. He's on the fence.

status_updates: Possesses the Babe Ruth signed ball

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Hercules uncovers 150 baseballs; the gang returns him to Mr. Mertle, who invites them in to talk baseball after learning they retrieved a ball from his yard.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Benny retrieves the Babe Ruth autograph and the gang discovers 150 baseballs.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benny obtains the autographed ball, the gang finds a stash of baseballs, and Mr. Mertle invites them to talk baseball after they return his dog.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Retrieve the Babe Ruth autographed ball.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benny has the autograph, the gang has 150 baseballs, and they are invited by Mr. Mertle to discuss baseball.
evidence_quotes: BENNY holding the Babe Ruth Autograph up high like a trophy. BENNY I GOT IT!
BENNY We can play forever now.
SCOTTY takes The Beast by the collar. He and Benny go to MR. MERTLE'S BACK DOOR Scotty KNOCKS.

relation_updates: Begins positive relationship with Mr. Mertle after returning Hercules.
status_updates: Possesses Babe Ruth autograph.
Gang has 150 baseballs for future games.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Triumphant upon achieving his goal of retrieving the autograph.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: After Benny defeats the Beast, his legend grows and he becomes known as Benny "The Juet" Rodriguez; the Sandlot empties as his sneaker lands on home plate.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Benny gains a lasting nickname and reputation after defeating the Beast.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After pickling the Beast, Benny's reputation spreads, and he is nicknamed 'Benny The Juet Rodriguez'.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benny is the only one left in the Sandlot, his sneaker lands on home plate, and his nickname is established.
evidence_quotes: Everyone but Benny has vanished from the Sandlot.
BENNY'S P.F. FLYER comes down on home plate and vanishes, leaving THE SANDLOT empty.

relation_updates: The Sandlot team disbands as members grow up and move on.
status_updates: Becomes known as Benny 'The Juet' Rodriguez
Reputation established as a neighborhood legend
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Nicknamed 'The Juet' for his feat against the Beast.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Benny Rodriguez begins by acknowledging new neighbor Scotty and decides to use baseball to integrate him into the sandlot gang. Through moral leadership, coaching, and crisis management, he guides the team through challenges, including a confrontation with 'The Beast' to retrieve a priceless autographed baseball. His actions cement his status as a legendary leader and friend, culminating in a lasting nickname and legacy.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: New kid in town recounts joining friends at a fountain where Scotty unknowingly drinks from a pee-contaminated spigot, causing disgust and laughter.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: New kid pranked by the group
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Scotty unknowingly drinks from the pee-contaminated spigot, leading the group to gag and faux-barf while he remains unaware.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Confused and laughed at by the group
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: His confusion about why the group is laughing
evidence_quotes: Scotty goes for the fountain.
Scotty drinks from the pee spigot!
Scotty has no idea why they're laughing at him.

relation_updates: Adversarial relationship with Ham, Squints, Benny, and Yeah-Yeah

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scotty starts fifth grade amid chaotic hallways, struggling to make friends before summer, marking the beginning of his story.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Student following a group of eight boys in the hallway
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Scotty spies a group of eight boys forging ahead in the chaotic hallway and follows them.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Following the group
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Limited time to make friends before summer
evidence_quotes: SCOTTY'S caught in the mayhem.
HE SPIES the 8 guys forging ahead.
He follows them.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scotty, a gifted but isolated boy, struggles with his mother's push to make friends and play outside; she encourages him to socialize, while he doubts himself, seeking connection and approval, including from his athlete father.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: A boy skilled with technical gadgets but socially isolated
timeline_nodes.salient_development: In his room, Scotty's mother confronts him about his isolation, urging him to make friends and go outside, which leads him to admit self-doubt and ask his father to teach him catch.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To learn catch from his father and make friends
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Scotty has agreed to try making friends and has asked his mother if his dad will teach him to play catch.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Scotty's self-doubt about being an 'egghead' and his ability to connect with peers
evidence_quotes: SCOTTY I know, but I don't - I'm no good at anything. Face it, Mom, I'm just an EGGHEAD
MOM Are you kidding, he'd love it, you know what an athlete he wasIn high school.

relation_updates: Mother pushes Scotty to socialize
Scotty seeks connection with his father by asking to learn catch
status_updates: Scotty admits to being an 'egghead' and not good at anything
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A smart boy who feels inadequate socially and seeks connection through his father

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scotty seeks connection with Bill by asking to learn catch; Bill responds indifferently, but later places a baseball on a pedestal, hinting at unspoken significance behind his distant behavior and the object's meaning.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Scotty asking Bill to teach him catch
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bill gives a vague 'Sure' to Scotty's request, leading to Scotty's disappointment and his observation of Bill placing a baseball on a pedestal.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To have Bill teach him catch
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Scotty leaves the room disappointed after seeing Bill set a baseball on a silver pedestal.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Why Bill placed the baseball on a pedestal and his distant behavior
evidence_quotes: Scotty tentatively addresses Bill, stumbling over his words as he asks if he remembers promising to teach him how to play catch.
Scotty, trying to mask his disappointment, says, 'Okay. Great. Thanks,' and turns to leave, quietly bringing the door with him.
Through the cracked opening, he sees Bill place a silver pedestal on the main shelf and carefully set a baseball on top

relation_updates: Bill responds indifferently to Scotty's request
Scotty feels disappointment
status_updates: Scotty leaves the room disappointed

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benny reveals Babe Ruth was called 'The Babe' because he cried when bullied at the orphanage, evoking empathy and leaving the boys silent as he exits.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Scotty tries to join the sandlot game but is rejected after a failed interaction.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Scotty is hit by a fly ball, throws it back weakly, and is laughed at by the gang, leading him to walk away crying.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To return the ball and be accepted into the game.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Scotty is humiliated and excluded, leaving the sandlot in tears.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Scotty will be accepted by the gang in the future.
evidence_quotes: SCOTTY whips a look up and sees THE FLY BALL coming right at him.
THE BALL beans him at the glove covering his noggin.
THE GANG LAUGHS uproariously.
SCOTTY walks away... crying.

relation_updates: The gang mocks Scotty for his poor throw, establishing an adversarial relationship
Benny does not laugh, showing potential empathy
status_updates: Scotty is rejected by the gang and leaves in tears
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Scotty is timid and easily embarrassed, as shown by his fear and reaction to the gang's mockery.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scotty, initially hesitant due to a black eye and no glove, accepts Benny's spare glove, agrees to play baseball, and confidently lies about using chewing tobacco as they plan to buy supplies.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Scotty as the invitee to a baseball game by Benny
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Scotty accepts Benny's invitation to play baseball and takes the spare glove offered, overcoming his initial hesitation due to his black eye.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To join Benny in playing baseball
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Scotty is walking with Benny to the 5 & 10 store to buy baseball supplies, now equipped with a spare glove.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Scotty's false claim about using chewing tobacco could be exposed later.
evidence_quotes: Benny crosses over to Scotty's sidewalk and invites him to play baseball, asking if he wants to join.
Scotty hesitates, declining politely, saying he doesn’t have a glove.
Benny reaches behind his back dramatically, as if drawing a weapon, then smiles and produces a spare 'real' glove, offering it to Scotty.
Scotty, completely clueless, confidently replies, 'Sure. A'course.'

relation_updates: Benny extends friendship by offering a glove and inviting Scotty to play
status_updates: Scotty gains a spare glove
Scotty has a black eye
Scotty lies about using chewing tobacco

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scotty sees a large, moving shape through a crack in the green panels, sparking fear after his initial success with the ball.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Novice right fielder in the sandlot game
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Scotty is assigned to right field, fails to catch a fly ball, is coached by Benny on throwing, and glimpses a large moving shape through the fence.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Scotty is on the field after receiving coaching from Benny, but now fearful due to seeing a shape in the sandlot.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Scotty can improve his baseball skills and what the shape he saw is.
evidence_quotes: Scotty trails after them.
SCOTTY (EAGER) Right. Okay. Ah... where exactly is that?
The ball lands 5 feet behind him.
BENNY Throw it like you chuck a paper. When your arm gets here... just let go of it.

relation_updates: Benny defends Scotty against team objections and coaches him
status_updates: Assigned to right field position in the game
Glimpses a large, moving shape through the fence

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scotty hesitates, then snatches Bill's baseball from its silver pedestal.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Scotty, who snatches Bill's baseball from its pedestal.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Scotty snatches Bill's baseball from its silver pedestal after hesitation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Scotty has taken Bill's baseball.
evidence_quotes: Scotty's HAND ENTERS FRAME.
His indecisive FINGERS almost touch the ball...
then, SNATCH!

status_updates: Scotty becomes a thief and now possesses the ball

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scotty collapses, prompting the boys to help; realizing the signed baseball is lost, they resolve to replace it. Benny leads efforts to collect bottles for 98 cents to buy a new ball, forging a fake Babe Ruth signature.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: The boy who hits the home run and loses the ball
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Scotty hits a home run, sending the signed Babe Ruth ball into the Beast's yard, which causes him to panic and unites the group to help.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To get the ball back before Scotty's stepfather finds out.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The group is united and aware of the crisis, with the ball in the Beast's yard.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to retrieve the ball from the Beast's yard.
evidence_quotes: SCOTTY We gotta get that ball back.
SCOTTY It's my stepdad's. I stole it from his trophy room.
DENUNEZ The Beast got it.

relation_updates: The gang unites to help Scotty replace the ball
status_updates: Scotty is in a state of panic and determination
The ball is in the Beast's yard

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Blind Mr. Mertle, a former baseball player and friend of Babe Ruth, offers Scotty a rare signed baseball in exchange for weekly baseball talks, replacing the chewed ball.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Scotty negotiates with Mr. Mertle to replace the chewed Babe Ruth ball.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Scotty trades the damaged ball for a rare 'Murderers' Row' ball and agrees to weekly baseball talks with Mr. Mertle.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Replace the chewed ball to avoid his stepdad's anger.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Has the new baseball and has committed to visiting Mr. Mertle weekly.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether his stepdad will accept the replacement ball.
evidence_quotes: SCOTTY Well, you see... that ball belongs to my stepdad... and, uh - it was signed by Babe Ruth.
MR. MERTLE I'll make you a deal. If you boys come over once a week and talk baseball with me, I'll trade you balls.
Scotty hands Mr. Mertle the chewed up Babe Ruth ball. Shakes his hand. SCOTTY Deal.

relation_updates: Establishes regular visit agreement with Mr. Mertle
status_updates: Now possesses a rare 'Murderers' Row' baseball

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scotty's mom calls them in for supper, but Bill, now a father figure, wants ten more minutes to help Scotty break in his new glove, continuing their bonding ritual.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Scotty plays catch with Bill, who is now called 'Dad'.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bill refers to Scotty as 'my boy' and is addressed as 'Dad' while helping break in Scotty's new glove.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Scotty continues playing catch with Bill, with Bill requesting more time to bond.
evidence_quotes: SMACK! it's caught in a brand new glove. Scotty's new glove.
Scotty snap throws the ball back to Bill.
Bill and Scotty smile as they fire the cowhide back and forth.
DAD (FORMERLY BILL) Give us another ten minutes. I'm helpin' my boy break in his new glove.

relation_updates: Bill fully embraces father role with Scotty
status_updates: Has new baseball glove
Father-son bond confirmed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Scotty is engaged in baseball activities with his father figure.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Scotty, a new kid in town, initially struggles with outsider status and seeks to make friends and connect with his stepfather Bill. After humiliations and setbacks, he is accepted into a baseball gang, but loses a priceless Babe Ruth baseball, leading to a crisis involving 'The Beast.' Through group efforts and a deal with Mr. Mertle, he replaces the ball and bonds with Bill, achieving familial and social integration.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benny joins the gang, who celebrate their win and head back to class, while new kid Scotty Smalls watches nervously as insults are exchanged.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: team statistician who tracks game stats and major league news
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Records the game's final score and announces a major league stolen base milestone
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Part of the undefeated Murderers' Row team, with game stats recorded and heading back to class
evidence_quotes: Squints jots the stats in his pee-chee folder.
SQUINTS Thirty-one! Maury Wills just stole number thirty-one!
SQUINTS Game over. Sixteen zip. Murderers' Row remains undefeated.

relation_updates: established positive team dynamic with Benny and the gang
status_updates: team statistician role confirmed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Attentive to baseball statistics and current major league events

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: New kid in town recounts joining friends at a fountain where Scotty unknowingly drinks from a pee-contaminated spigot, causing disgust and laughter.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: group participant in drinking fountain prank
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Squints joins Ham and Benny in avoiding the pee-contaminated spigot and participates in mocking Scotty Smalls when he drinks from it.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: part of group that excludes Scotty Smalls from knowledge of the prank
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Scotty Smalls remains unaware of why the group is laughing at him
evidence_quotes: Just as Ham, Squints and Benny go for the 3 spigots,
Everyone drinks from the 2 "un-cursed" spigots.
The guys hang - waiting for doom.
The guys GAG and FAUX-BARF.

relation_updates: participates in group mockery of Scotty Smalls

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benny reveals Babe Ruth was called 'The Babe' because he cried when bullied at the orphanage, evoking empathy and leaving the boys silent as he exits.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: participant who offers incorrect baseball history
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Squints states an incorrect theory that Babe Ruth was called 'The Babe' because he was like 'the child of Yankee Stadium', but Benny corrects him with a story about Ruth's childhood bullying at an orphanage, leaving the group silent.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: part of the group left silent after Benny's correction and empathy lesson
evidence_quotes: SQUINTS Wrong. The Babe was called The Babe, because he was like the child of Yankee Stadium.

relation_updates: receives correction from Benny about baseball history

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scotty sees a large, moving shape through a crack in the green panels, sparking fear after his initial success with the ball.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: team member opposing new player Scotty
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Squints insults Scotty as an 'L-7 weenie' and is mocked by Benny for catching and running poorly.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Squints is defensive and the team proceeds to play with Scotty in right field.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Mocking from Benny about Squints' baseball skills.
evidence_quotes: SQUINTS No you don't. It's stupid, Benny. The kid's an L-7 weenie.
BENNY Oh yeah, Squints, and you're Willie freakin' Mays. You catch like a dork - anybody ever bust your chops about that?

relation_updates: Conflict with Benny over team membership and baseball abilities
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Critical but easily silenced when challenged.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Squints prepares to tell the legendary story of The Beast from Mertle's Acres, claiming it caused extreme reactions in those who heard it before, demanding silence and attention from the group.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: storyteller beginning the legend of The Beast
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Squints starts narrating the scary story, claiming past extreme reactions from the group and demanding silence.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The group is silent and attentive as the story begins.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The full story of The Beast is yet to be told.
evidence_quotes: Squints turns the lantern down low...
SQUINTS Alright, listen up.
First time DeNunez heard this story he FAINTED
When Yeah-Yeah heard it he peed his pants.

relation_updates: Asserts authority as storyteller to the group
status_updates: Becomes the designated narrator for the legend

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Squints and Yeah-Yeah buy a baseball; Squints removes the shine while being distracted by a beautiful blonde girl who smiles at him, leaving him flustered.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: first-time observer smitten by Wendy Peffercorn
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Squints sees Wendy Peffercorn, stares and ogles at her, and is left grinning goofily after she smiles.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Squints is flustered and grinning in response to Wendy's smile.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Squints' crush on Wendy begins, leaving him visibly affected.
evidence_quotes: His eyes go suddenly wide and he stares, and oggles, and gawks
She smiles at Squints.
SQUINTS can only manage a totally embarrassing goofy grin.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Squints, overwhelmed by his infatuation with lifeguard Wendy, fakes drowning to be rescued by her, but ruins it by kissing her, leading to his humiliation and ejection from the pool.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: infatuated pool-goer who fakes drowning to get Wendy's attention
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Fakes drowning to be rescued by Wendy, kisses her impulsively, and is humiliated and ejected from the pool.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to get Wendy's attention and affection
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Squints is called a pervert and removed from the pool by Wendy.
evidence_quotes: SQUINTS holds his nose. Takes the deep leap.
Squints can no longer restrain himself, he grabs her - gives her a sloppy SMOOCH!
LIFEGUARD You little pervert! She grabs Squints by the scruff of the neck. Run-walks him toward the exit, and...

relation_updates: Wendy Peffercorn now sees Squints as a pervert and rejects him.
status_updates: Squints is ejected from the pool.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Squints kissed the lifeguard, got banned from the pool, but earned her lasting smile whenever they passed by afterward, impressing his friends with his bold act.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: the boy who kissed the lifeguard
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Squints admits to planning the kiss for years, kisses the lifeguard, and is banned from the pool, but the lifeguard smiles at him afterward and his friends show increased respect.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Banned from the pool forever, with the lifeguard smiling at Squints from her watchtower when they pass by, and peers tipping their hats to him.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Permanently banned from the public pool.
evidence_quotes: SQUINTS A'course I did. Been plannin' it for years.
We got banned from the pool forever that day...
the Lifeguard looked down from her watchtower, right over at Squints... and smiled.

relation_updates: Lifeguard now smiles at Squints from her watchtower
Peers show increased respect and admiration for Squints
status_updates: Kissed a woman for the first time
Gains confidence and walks taller after the act
auxiliary.persona_anchor: the boy who planned and executed a bold kiss on the lifeguard

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The kids, lacking ideas, agree to assess the situation by observing Mr. Mertle's yard, inspired by Squints' suggestion to survey the environment like in 'Combat.'
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Group leader in the tree house meeting who proposes a plan to assess Mr. Mertle's yard.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Admits having no idea, then suggests the group survey the enemy's environment, citing inspiration from the TV show 'Combat.'
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To observe Mr. Mertle's yard to figure out how to retrieve the lost baseball.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The group agrees to look out the window to assess the situation.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to actually retrieve the ball after the assessment.
evidence_quotes: SQUINTS I have no idea.
SQUINTS We need to assess the situation!
SQUINTS First we survey the enemy's environment, then we make note of the surrounding terrain.

relation_updates: Tense dynamic with Ham when questioned about the plan
status_updates: Assumes leadership role in strategy meeting
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Uses pop culture references ('Combat') to formulate and justify plans.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Squints reappears covered in dust, removes his glasses, and proposes tunneling for a surprise attack, admitting prior mistakes.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: proposes tunneling strategy after explosion
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Appears covered in dust, admits prior mistakes, and suggests tunneling for surprise attack.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To achieve total surprise by tunneling against the Beast.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Tunneling plan proposed to the gang.
evidence_quotes: Squints appears.
He's battleship grey.
Takes his glasses off. Only his eyes show white.

relation_updates: Reaffirms cooperative stance with the gang by using 'we'
status_updates: Covered in dust from the explosion
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Admits personal fault and uses 'we' to emphasize group strategy.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: After Benny defeats the Beast, his legend grows and he becomes known as Benny "The Juet" Rodriguez; the Sandlot empties as his sneaker lands on home plate.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: childhood friend whose future is narrated
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Squints' future is disclosed in the narrator's summary: he marries Wendy Peffercorn and they purchase the Five & Dime.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Married to Wendy Peffercorn and co-owner of the Five & Dime, as revealed in narration.
evidence_quotes: Squints grew up and married Wendy Peffercorn; they bought the Five & Dime and they still own it to this day.

relation_updates: married Wendy Peffercorn
status_updates: became co-owner of the Five & Dime store

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Squints begins as the team's statistician and a group participant in hazing, showing baseball knowledge and occasional conflict with Benny. He asserts authority through storytelling about The Beast and develops a crush on Wendy Peffercorn, culminating in a bold kiss that leads to both humiliation and admiration. Later, he takes on strategic roles in group plans and eventually marries Wendy, co-owning the Five & Dime.
